scope.load: replay ~1959 book msgs/min, representative of OBSERVED 60-MINUTE LOAD (WO-008b-B-RERUN)
scope.duration: replay 60.0s establishment (pinned WO-009 source, pinned rate ~1959/min, same host, same protocol)
scope.load_work: per-frame PROCESSING COST characterized by the pinned WO-009 fixture frame shapes (SNAPSHOT_FRAME + UPDATE_MODIFY_LEVEL), INCLUDING WO-017 wire-string retention (per-level WireDecimal construction at parse + .wire consumption at checksum, replacing the interim per-level format(x,'f') render). Empirical justification: live-vs-replay agreement at +0.008 ms, representative as measured. INVALIDATION CONDITION: deeper ladders, heavier per-level validation, or additional per-level storage require re-validation (WO-017 §6 — this WO's own change was that condition's first trigger).
scope.resolution: NOISE FLOOR of the establishment instrument (fifth scope dimension, WO-013 follow-up A / 0.1j). WIDENED (full-loop) instrument, this host (WO-013 B, now the DEFAULT): within-session core ~0.2 ms (1 sigma), plus heavier tails than adapter-only (an observed +1.6 ms outlier in 4 runs), so OPERATIONAL FLOOR ~1.5 ms (provisional, n=4). ADAPTER-ONLY (legacy subset): within-session ~0.3 ms (1 sigma), CROSS-SESSION ~1.0 ms (WO-017 107.961 vs WO-013 108.979 ms on byte-identical adapter code). STANDING REPORT FORM for every future delta: SIGNAL=|new-baseline|, NOISE=floor, RATIO=SIGNAL/NOISE; RATIO<1 => record with SIGN EXPLICITLY UNESTABLISHED and KEEP the ledger entry (it BOUNDS the effect). Derivations: evidence/WO-013/noise_floor_and_ab_report.txt (A) and widened_instrument.txt (B).
scope.instrument: The stored mean_cycle_seconds (0.107923) was measured on the ADAPTER-ONLY instrument (process_raw_frame). WO-013 follow-up B WIDENED the instrument to the FULL LOOP ITERATION (adapter + strategy.decide + risk.check + emission), so rule text and instrument coverage name the same boundary. The widened instrument measures ~108.7 ms core (108.519/108.711/108.912/110.303 ms), a +0.79 ms delta vs the stored figure at RATIO 0.79 — BELOW the noise floor, sign unestablished: loop overhead is below resolution (consistent with WO-017's +0.008 ms live-vs-replay agreement). The stored figure is RETAINED (not re-declared to a below-floor, different-instrument number) and remains a valid live-gate reference (the live gate already measures the full loop; 50% drift-VOID makes sub-ms immaterial). Instrument HENCEFORTH widened (establish tool default).

ba47c96a36f36642.derivation: establishment protocol: 1959 frames replayed at 1959/min over 60s from tests/fixtures/kraken_v2_raw_frames.py (WO-009 pinned source); mean_cycle=span/actual. RE-BASELINED under WO-017 (wire-string retention), the first pre-declared, legitimate re-baseline (§5): executed inside the WO that caused the pipeline change, before the changed code faces any live gate.
ba47c96a36f36642.load: replay ~1959 book msgs/min (representative of WO-008b-B-RERUN observed load); source run-id WO-008b-B-RERUN-20260721T170944Z; duration 60s; material-diff trigger: a sustained rate outside +-20% of 1959/min (<1567 or >2351/min) is MATERIALLY DIFFERENT and re-declares the representative rate, dated, old scope annotated.

rebaseline.pipeline_change: WO-017 wire-string retention: _current_ladder_strings now returns the venue's retained WireDecimal.wire text; the per-level format(x,'f') render (WO-016 INTERIM fix) is removed.
rebaseline.attributed_to: wire-string retention replacing per-level fixed-point rendering (a .wire attribute read is cheaper than format(x,'f') per level). Three establishment runs at 1959/min/60s: 107.732, 107.923, 107.923 ms (range ~0.2%); the -0.9% shift is reproducible, not run-to-run noise.
rebaseline.material: NOT material to any gate (well within the 50% mean-cycle drift-VOID bound and the +-20% rate scope), but a REAL, reproducible, FAVORABLE change: the structural fix is also marginally cheaper per cycle. The 'at what cost' clause is answered in milliseconds under identical replayed load: the cost is negative.
rebaseline.noise_floor_annotation_2026-07-22: Recorded, SIGN NOT ESTABLISHED. Measured across sessions; WO-013's establishment runs subsequently demonstrated cross-session variation of comparable magnitude on unchanged code. The mechanism (removing a per-cycle rendering pass) remains plausible; this measurement does not establish it. The honest reading is 'wire-string retention costs approximately nothing' — which satisfies the feasibility pre-ruling's bar of ACCEPTABLE cost. The letter-closure of FR-018a(f) never rested on the sign. [SIGNAL 0.963 ms / NOISE 1.0 ms / RATIO 0.96 -> inside the floor. Annotated per WO-013 follow-up A.3, never rewritten.]

assessments.pipeline_change: WO-013 reason-code emission: loop (live.py) emits the DECLARED codes NO_SIGNAL / LONG_SIGNAL / SHORT_SIGNAL and KillSwitchEngagedError defaults to KILL_SWITCH_ENGAGED.
assessments.result: BASELINE HOLDS at 0.107923s — NO re-declaration. Rationale: (1) the establishment tool measures the ADAPTER book-apply cycle (process_raw_frame), which WO-013 does not touch — the adapter is byte-identical to WO-017; (2) WO-013's loop change RENAMED an already-existing per-frame emission (STRAT_NO_SIGNAL -> NO_SIGNAL, byte-identical log_decision call) and added two RARELY-TRIGGERED emissions (LONG/SHORT_SIGNAL on signal, KILL_SWITCH_ENGAGED on a blocked order) — ZERO new per-frame work. Establishment runs measured 109.305/108.910/108.721 ms, differing from 107.923 by cross-session environmental NOISE (same-host, different day), NOT attributable to this change. A new figure pinned to reason-code emission would be false attribution of noise. Hot-path standing rule was APPLIED (work done), and the honest finding is: no per-frame cost added. Evidence: evidence/WO-013/rebaseline.txt.

assessments.pipeline_change: WO-013 follow-up B — INSTRUMENT WIDENED to the full loop iteration (adapter + strategy.decide + risk.check + emission), replacing the adapter-only process_raw_frame instrument so rule scope == instrument scope.
assessments.result: BASELINE HOLDS at 0.107923s (adapter-only), scope annotated 'adapter-only'; instrument henceforth WIDENED (default). Widened establishment (4x 60s, ~1957/min): 108.519/108.711/108.912/110.303 ms, core mean 108.714 ms. Widened vs stored: SIGNAL 0.791 ms / NOISE 1.0 ms / RATIO 0.79 -> INSIDE THE FLOOR, sign unestablished; the widened core is even slightly BELOW the same-session adapter-only mean (108.714 vs 108.979 ms), i.e. loop overhead is below the noise floor (consistent with WO-017's +0.008 ms live-vs-replay). NOT re-declared to a below-floor, different-instrument figure (A.2). Widened noise floor re-established: within-session core ~0.2 ms (1 sigma), heavier tails (an outlier +1.6 ms), operational ~1.5 ms provisional (n=4). Closure: rule text (progress.md) and instrument (establish tool default) now name the full loop. Evidence: evidence/WO-013/widened_instrument.txt.

superseded.end_reason: Superseded by the WO-017 wire-string-retention re-baseline (0.107923s). NOT overwritten: retained here as the baseline ledger's prior entry so the pipeline's performance evolution stays traceable (no orphan figures).
